Hero.title: Unheard India
Hero.subtitle: Documenting the diverse cultural heritage of India's marginalized communities.

Footer.tagline: Preserving stories today, so history doesn't lose them tomorrow.

About.title: The School of Design at DIT University
About.subtitle: A multidisciplinary creative space where ideas, research, and innovation come together.
About.introText: The department brings together diverse design domains including:
About.quote: At SOD, design is not just about aesthetics; it is about understanding people, contexts, cultures, and systems before creating meaningful solutions.
About.ethnoP1: The ethnographic research showcased on this platform has been conducted by students of the UX Department, where human behavior, lived experiences, and contextual inquiry form the foundation of the design process.
About.ethnoP2: Through field immersion, interviews, observation, and documentation, students explore real-world narratives and translate them into actionable insights and impactful design interventions.
About.disclaimerP1: This website showcases academic work developed by students of the School of Design (SOD), DIT University, as part of their coursework and research requirements under faculty supervision.
About.disclaimerP2: All documentaries, research findings, and visual documentation presented here are created solely for academic and educational purposes. The interpretations, narratives, and representations reflect students' research-based exploration within a structured academic framework.
About.disclaimerP3: The content does not represent official institutional statements, endorsements, or policy positions of DIT University. It is intended to document and present student-led academic research and learning outcomes.

Communities.title: Communities We Document
Communities.subtitle: Lives, crafts, and cultures that shape India's diverse heritage.
Communities.noResultsDesc: We couldn't find any matches for "{searchTerm}" in {region}.

Contact.title: Collaborate With Us
Contact.subtitle: For partnerships, research collaboration, and meaningful engagement
Contact.introText: We welcome collaboration with organizations and individuals committed to preserving cultural heritage, supporting marginalized communities, and advancing ethical ethnographic research.
Contact.ngosDesc: If you work directly with these or similar communities and are interested in collaborative documentation, advocacy, or program development.
Contact.academicDesc: Universities and research institutions interested in ethnographic collaboration, joint publications, or accessing research materials for academic purposes.
Contact.governmentDesc: Government departments working on tribal welfare, cultural preservation, livelihood development, or inclusive policy design.
Contact.mediaDesc: Filmmakers, journalists, and content creators committed to ethical storytelling and authentic representation of marginalized communities.
Contact.getInTouchDesc: We carefully consider each collaboration opportunity to ensure alignment with our values of dignity, respect, and meaningful impact.
Contact.emailNote: Please include details about your organization and how you envision working together.
Contact.principle1: All collaborations must center community benefit and dignity
Contact.principle2: We do not support extractive research or exploitative documentation
Contact.principle3: Commercial use of content requires explicit permission and benefit-sharing agreements
Contact.principle4: We prioritize long-term engagement over one-time interventions
Contact.principle5: Transparency, ethical practice, and mutual respect are non-negotiable

Documentaries.title: Documentary Library
Documentaries.subtitle: Visual ethnography: Watch the stories that need to be told.

Submission.title: Submit Your Research
Submission.subtitle: Share your ethnographic research, thesis, publications, or field data with the academic community.
Submission.selectPrompt: What would you like to submit?
Submission.thesisDesc: Submit your completed thesis or dissertation.
Submission.publicationDesc: Share your journal articles or conference papers.
Submission.datasetDesc: Contribute primary research data or raw field notes.
Submission.maxFileSize: Maximum file size: 500MB • Accepted formats: PDF, ZIP
Submission.supportingMediaDesc: Videos, audio, or images related to your dataset
Submission.ethicsUploadDesc: IRB approval, community consent forms, participant agreements
Submission.declaration: I declare that this research was conducted in accordance with ethical guidelines, all participants provided informed consent, and the data is accurate to the best of my knowledge. I understand that this submission will undergo faculty review before publication.
Submission.guideline1: Ensure all personal identifiers are properly anonymized where required
Submission.guideline2: Include complete metadata (keywords, abstract, community details)
Submission.guideline3: Ethics approval documentation is mandatory for all submissions
Submission.guideline4: Large datasets (>500MB) may require special upload arrangements
Submission.guideline5: Faculty review typically takes 3-5 business days
Submission.successDesc: Thank you for contributing to Unheard India. Your research has been sent for faculty review.
Submission.errorUploadEthics: Please upload the ethics approval form.

Ethics.title: Ethics & Privacy Guidelines
Ethics.subtitle: Our research framework is built on the principle of 'Dignity over Documentation'. We follow strict anthropological ethics to protect marginalized voices.
Ethics.consentDesc: Prior to any recording or interview, participants are briefed in their native language about the research goals and their right to withdraw at any time.
Ethics.anonymDesc: Sensitive field data, specifically involving legal status or internal community conflicts, is strictly anonymized to prevent any real-world harm.
Ethics.benefitDesc: We ensure that research findings are shared back with the community, and any commercial use of craft imagery is strictly regulated.
Ethics.governanceIntro: As an aspiring Full-Stack Developer and researcher, ensuring data integrity is paramount. The Ethnography Research Documentation Project operates under the following governance structure:
Ethics.visualEthics: Faces of minors are blurred unless explicit parental consent is provided.
Ethics.dataSovereignty: Communities retain the right to request the removal of specific oral histories from the public archive.
Ethics.safeStorage: Raw field notes are stored in encrypted MongoDB instances, accessible only by verified faculty members.

Research.title: Research & Academic Work
Research.subtitle: A collection of scholarly work documenting, analyzing, and preserving the cultural heritage and lived experiences of marginalized communities through rigorous ethnographic research
Research.impactQuote: Our research contributes to academic discourse on cultural anthropology, social justice, and heritage preservation while directly supporting community visibility, policy advocacy, and dignified representation.
Research.fieldDataDesc: primary datasets, interviews, photographs, and field documentation. Open for academic use.
Research.submitDesc: Share your thesis, publications, or field data with the academic community. Contribute to the growing body of knowledge.
Research.facultyDesc: Review student submissions and manage the research repository
